nodes.label: Tenant Scoping Mechanism Decision Table
nodes.rationale: JWT claim is most likely mechanism given IdentityServer setup; no extra work needed if so, since token already proves tenant identity.

nodes.label: Common Field Translation Patterns (API to Domain)
nodes.norm_label: common field translation patterns (api to domain)

nodes.label: Feature Vertical Slice Pattern
nodes.rationale: A full slice from domain entity through use cases, infrastructure repository, presentation hook, and page component ensures consistent architecture per feature.

nodes.label: Mock Strategy Per Layer Table
nodes.rationale: Never mix mock strategies: use fakes for use cases, MSW for infrastructure boundary, fake container for presentation, to keep tests aligned with what layer they verify.

nodes.label: erasableSyntaxOnly Test Gotcha
nodes.rationale: Applies to all classes including test helpers and fakes: no constructor parameter shorthand allowed even though vitest alone would pass.

nodes.label: exactOptionalPropertyTypes Conditional Spread Gotcha
nodes.rationale: Optional fields must be conditionally spread rather than assigned directly to satisfy strict optional property typing.

nodes.label: onUnhandledRequest: error Config
nodes.rationale: Any unhandled request fails loudly, catching missing handlers rather than silently hanging tests.

nodes.label: react-hooks/set-state-in-effect Suppression
nodes.rationale: useAsync's void execute() call inside useEffect triggers this rule as a false positive since the rule traces async call graphs; suppression is intentional and documented, must not be removed or reused elsewhere.

nodes.label: erasableSyntaxOnly Constraint
nodes.rationale: No constructor parameter property shorthand; explicit field declaration and assignment in constructor body required.

nodes.label: exactOptionalPropertyTypes Constraint
nodes.rationale: Requires explicit undefined checks before assigning optional fields, never direct assignment of possibly-undefined values.

nodes.label: noUncheckedIndexedAccess Constraint
nodes.rationale: Index access returns T | undefined so all index accesses must be guarded.

nodes.label: Clean Architecture Boundary Constraint
nodes.rationale: domain/ and application/ must never import React, react-router, or infrastructure/presentation; ESLint enforces this boundary.

nodes.label: TenantContext First Param Rule
nodes.rationale: Every repository interface method takes TenantContext as first param to structurally enforce tenant scoping in the application layer.

nodes.label: Tenant Scoping via JWT Claim
nodes.rationale: The tenant_id claim inside the JWT access token is read by the backend directly, so AuthenticatedHttpClient does not need a separate tenant header; TenantContext param remains for structural enforcement in the application layer.

nodes.label: How to Add a New Resource Workflow
nodes.norm_label: how to add a new resource workflow

nodes.label: erasableSyntaxOnly Decision
nodes.rationale: Forces explicit field declarations instead of constructor parameter shorthand, aligning with Clean Code principles at the cost of verbosity, applied project-wide.

nodes.label: exactOptionalPropertyTypes Decision
nodes.rationale: Kept despite friction because domain models auth session/tenant context where field-absent vs explicitly-undefined are meaningfully different states; caught real bugs during Auth build.

nodes.label: noUncheckedIndexedAccess Decision
nodes.rationale: Motivated by oidcUser.profile['tenant_id'] resolving to unknown not string; runtime guard typeof x === 'string' is the only safe approach.

nodes.label: oidc-client-ts Library Decision
nodes.rationale: Framework-agnostic, maintained standard for Auth Code + PKCE flows with full TypeScript types.

nodes.label: automaticSilentRenew: false Decision
nodes.rationale: Event-driven background renewal is invisible to callers who cannot observe failure or trigger logout; explicit renewal in getCurrentSession() gives full control.

nodes.label: HandleAuthCallback Propagates Errors Unwrapped Decision
nodes.rationale: Exact error shapes from oidc-client-ts on various failure modes were unknown at build time; wrapping prematurely would discard information the presentation layer might need. Revisit when IdentityServer backend exists and real error shapes observed.

nodes.label: tenant_id Claim Name Decision
nodes.rationale: Used as claim name in IdentityServer tokens; only referenced in oidcUserToSessionMapper.ts; revisit when real token can be decoded and claim name confirmed.

nodes.label: email and name on User Entity Decision
nodes.rationale: Included as optional fields marked as unverified assumptions since actual IdentityServer configuration not confirmed.
nodes.norm_label: email and name on user entity decision

nodes.label: No Server-State Library Decision
nodes.rationale: Explicit project constraint from the brief; most state is server data scoped by tenant with no complex shared client state or advanced caching requirements in v1.

nodes.label: useAsync immediate Flag Decision
nodes.rationale: Defaults to true for fetch-on-mount pattern; immediate:false covers action-style mutation calls triggered by user interaction.

nodes.label: set-state-in-effect Suppression Decision
nodes.rationale: Documented false positive per react/react#34743; the rule traces async call graphs and flags setState after await though those calls are genuinely async.

nodes.label: MSW onUnhandledRequest error Decision
nodes.rationale: Silent failures like hanging promises are worse than loud ones; unhandled requests must fail immediately.

nodes.label: No Real Network Calls in Tests Decision
nodes.rationale: UserManager does its own internal fetch management so MSW (which intercepts fetch) can't mock it; a hand-written fake createFakeUserManager() is used instead.
nodes.norm_label: no real network calls in tests decision

nodes.label: Context-based DI (not module-level singletons) Decision
nodes.rationale: Singleton modules make testing harder via shared state between tests; context-based DI lets each test provide its own fake container without module-level mocking.

nodes.label: ProtectedRoute Handles Loading State Explicitly Decision
nodes.rationale: Prevents a race condition where an authenticated user gets briefly redirected to login before getCurrentSession() resolves.
nodes.norm_label: protectedroute handles loading state explicitly decision

nodes.label: Presentation Design Language Decision
nodes.rationale: Slate gray primary, white surfaces, teal accent chosen to feel calm, professional, trustworthy for healthcare/wellness business owners rather than startup-flashy.

nodes.label: Recommended Build Order
nodes.rationale: HttpClient unblocks all REST features; Services first as simplest CRUD with no dependencies; Appointments depends on Services; Inbox depends on Clients; Dashboard depends on Appointments and Inbox; Settings independent.

nodes.label: ADR 001: Clean Architecture Layer Structure
nodes.rationale: Strict inward-only dependency rule (domain <- application <- infrastructure + composition + presentation) keeps domain and use case logic framework-agnostic and testable in isolation; swapping IdentityServer, HTTP client, or router requires changes only in outer layers.

nodes.label: ADR 002: No Server-State Library
nodes.rationale: Explicit project constraint; most state is server data scoped by tenant with no complex shared client state, optimistic updates, or advanced caching needs in v1; migrate to TanStack Query if caching requirements grow.

nodes.label: ADR 003: Manual Dependency Injection, No Container Library
nodes.rationale: Dependency graph is simple and static; a DI library would add decorators conflicting with erasableSyntaxOnly and unneeded indirection at this scale; reconsider beyond ~10 repositories.
nodes.norm_label: adr 003: manual dependency injection, no container library

nodes.label: ADR 004: Explicit Silent Token Renewal, Not Event-Driven
nodes.rationale: automaticSilentRenew:false because event-driven background renewal fires invisibly with no clean hook to force logout on failure; explicit renewal inside getCurrentSession() gives full control over retry, session clearing, and redirect.
nodes.norm_label: adr 004: explicit silent token renewal, not event-driven
